title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP · Transfer Build
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers threat models, untrusted content, xss, paths, templates, and csp into a changed explicit static-publishing trust and security policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of explicit static-publishing trust and security policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.
Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Threat Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 3699 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Threat Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Threat Model, keep Threat Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-defense-in-depth-1orpl3
# Competency: ssg-defense-in-depth.
# Case 3738: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_defense_in_depth_1orpl3(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1orpl3 = "1orpl3-3lpro1-3738"
    assert evidence_variant_1orpl3
    evidence_axes_1orpl3 = (
        "reader1orpl3",
        "source3738",
        "parse3lpro1",
        "route1orpl33738",
        "artifact37381orpl3",
        "recovery3lpro13738",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1orpl3)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"
